---
title: "Error Handling"
description: "Handle template build and validation errors in the Go SDK."
---

# Error Handling

Template APIs in the Go SDK return ordinary `error` values. Unlike the JS/Python docs, Go callers inspect the error chain with `errors.As(...)`.

Template-related flows can surface:

- `*e2b.AuthenticationError`
- `*e2b.GitAuthError`
- `*e2b.TemplateError`
- `*e2b.BuildError`
- `*e2b.FileUploadError`

## Go-specific behavior

- `Build(...)`, `BuildInBackground(...)`, `GetBuildStatus(...)`, `Exists(...)`, `AssignTags(...)`, and `RemoveTags(...)` return `error`
- `FileUploadError` also matches `BuildError`
- `BuildError` may include `CallerTrace`
- some builder validations fail immediately by panicking with `*e2b.BuildError` instead of returning an error later

## Match template build errors

Use `errors.As(...)` to distinguish authentication problems, upload failures, and build failures.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"log"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()
	template := e2b.Template(nil).FromBaseImage()

	_, err := e2b.Build(ctx, template, "my-template", nil)
	if err == nil {
		return
	}

	var authErr *e2b.AuthenticationError
	var uploadErr *e2b.FileUploadError
	var buildErr *e2b.BuildError

	switch {
	case errors.As(err, &authErr):
		log.Printf("authentication failed: %s\n", authErr.Message)
	case errors.As(err, &uploadErr):
		log.Printf("file upload failed: %s\n", uploadErr.Message)
	case errors.As(err, &buildErr):
		log.Printf("build failed: %s\n", buildErr.Message)
	default:
		log.Printf("unexpected template error: %v\n", err)
	}
}
```

`FileUploadError` unwraps to `BuildError`, so one error can match both levels.

## Inspect caller trace

When a build fails on a specific template step, `BuildError.CallerTrace` can tell you which builder call site produced the failing step.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"log"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()

	_, err := e2b.Build(ctx, e2b.Template(nil).FromTemplate("this-template-does-not-exist"), "my-template", nil)
	if err == nil {
		return
	}

	var buildErr *e2b.BuildError
	if errors.As(err, &buildErr) {
		log.Printf("build failed: %s\n", buildErr.Message)
		if buildErr.CallerTrace != "" {
			log.Printf("caller trace:\n%s\n", buildErr.CallerTrace)
		}
	}
}
```

This is especially useful for:

- failed remote template bases
- failed build steps returned by the control plane
- local file hashing or upload failures tied to a `Copy(...)` call

## Handle template helper validation errors

Some template helper APIs return `*e2b.TemplateError` when the input shape is invalid.

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"log"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.Background()

	_, err := e2b.AssignTags(ctx, "tmpl:latest", 123, nil)
	if err == nil {
		return
	}

	var templateErr *e2b.TemplateError
	if errors.As(err, &templateErr) {
		log.Printf("template validation failed: %s\n", templateErr.Message)
	}
}
```

Examples of current validation that can return `TemplateError`:

- `Build(...)` or `BuildInBackground(...)` without a template name or legacy alias
- `GetBuildStatus(...)` with `nil` `BuildInfo`
- `AssignTags(...)` or `RemoveTags(...)` with a tags value that is not `string` or `[]string`

## Recover from immediate builder validation failures

Some template-builder methods validate local input immediately and panic with `*e2b.BuildError`. For example, `Copy(...)` rejects absolute paths and `..` escapes in the source path.

```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"log"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	defer func() {
		recovered := recover()
		if recovered == nil {
			return
		}

		err, ok := recovered.(error)
		if !ok {
			panic(recovered)
		}

		var buildErr *e2b.BuildError
		if errors.As(err, &buildErr) {
			log.Printf("builder validation failed: %s\n", buildErr.Message)
			return
		}

		panic(recovered)
	}()

	e2b.Template(nil).FromBaseImage().Copy("/absolute/path", "/app", nil)
}
```

This pattern is mainly useful for wrappers, tooling, or tests. In ordinary application code, the simpler approach is to avoid invalid local paths up front.

For the full exported error surface, see the [Go SDK Errors reference](/docs/sdk-reference/go-sdk/errors).
